name: two distinct owned items collide on the owns identity key
description: The headline ownership failure, now fixed. The structural identity key was (subject, entity, predicate, qualifier) -- pipeline._existing_facts did not filter on object_entity_id -- so owning a Civic and a kayak, both emitted as state-kind Me.owns with an empty qualifier, shared one identity key and the second insert (kayak) superseded the Civic with a spurious fact_conflict. Spec-correct (docs/reference/ANALYSIS.md 'property graph': me.owns -> [thing] is keyed by the thing; distinct things are distinct edges): both edges stay active, no chain, no review. Fixed by adding object_entity_id to the candidate read for non-functional predicates (functional predicates keep a single key across objects so a new employer still supersedes the old). Probe both notes use qualifier='' to mimic a model that did not coin a per-item qualifier.
